title: 10 best herbal decoctions for fluid retention
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for fluid retention are natural remedies made by steeping herbs in hot water to create a concentrated liquid that helps reduce excess fluid buildup in the body. These decoctions work by stimulating urine production, improving circulation, and relaxing the muscles, all of which help alleviate symptoms such as bloating, swelling, and discomfort. Examples of herbal decoctions that effectively combat fluid retention include dandelion root, peppermint leaf, and corn silk tea. By incorporating these remedies into their daily routine, individuals can experience improved energy levels, reduced bloating, and a sense of overall well-being, ultimately enhancing the quality of their lives.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle decoctions helps with fluid retention because they contain a unique combination of compounds that target the underlying causes of this issue. The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and alleviate pressure on the lymphatic system, allowing it to function more efficiently in removing excess fluids from the body. Additionally, nettle's diuretic properties increase urine production, encouraging the kidneys to flush out retained water and electrolytes, leading to a significant reduction in fluid retention.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Stinging nettle decoctions can increase urine production, helping to remove excess fluids and electrolytes from the body, thus reducing swelling and water retention.

Anti-inflammatory and anti-allergic effects: Nettle's anti-inflammatory and anti-allergic compounds can reduce inflammation and alleviate allergic reactions that may contribute to fluid retention, such as histamine-induced edema.
Vasodilatory properties: Stinging nettle decoctions can dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing pressure on the kidneys, which can help reduce fluid buildup in tissues.
remedy_constituents: Stachydrine: Acts as a diuretic, increasing urine production to help eliminate excess fluids from the body.
Histamine: Although often associated with allergic reactions, histamine in Urtica dioica also has a role in reducing fluid retention by increasing urine production and improving blood flow.
Alkaloids (specifically, caffeic acid and rosmarinic acid): Have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms of fluid retention.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are a common choice due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to their diuretic properties.
Roots: Roots are used for their ability to promote lymphatic drainage and reduce fluid retention, thanks to their rich content of saponins and other bioactive compounds.
Stems: Stems are another popular option due to their high levels of vitamin C, flavonoids, and other nutrients that support kidney function and promote the elimination of excess fluids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried urtica dioica leaves and stems from a reputable source.
Crush the dried urtica dioica leaves and stems into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powder with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes over low heat to release the active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with fluid retention because of its unique ability to stimulate the kidneys and promote diuretic functions. The herb's leaves, roots, and flowers contain compounds that increase urine production, allowing excess fluids to be eliminated from the body more efficiently. This natural diuresis can help alleviate symptoms of water retention, such as bloating and swelling, and provide relief for individuals struggling with conditions like edema or hypertension. Regular consumption of dandelion decoctions can also help maintain healthy blood pressure levels and promote overall kidney function.
remedy_properties: Diuretic effect: Dandelion roots and leaves contain compounds that stimulate urine production, helping to remove excess fluids and reduce swelling. This diuretic effect helps to alleviate symptoms of fluid retention.

Antioxidant activity: Dandelion contains antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation associated with fluid retention. By reducing inflammation, dandelion decoctions can help ease swelling and discomfort.

Choleretic effect: Dandelion roots contain compounds that stimulate bile production in the liver, helping to improve digestion and reduce water retention by breaking down fats more efficiently. This choleretic effect helps to alleviate symptoms of fluid retention and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: A triterpenoid saponin that helps reduce fluid retention by inhibiting the reabsorption of sodium and water in the kidneys, thereby promoting diuresis.
Taraxasterol acetate: A triterpenoid saponin that helps alleviate fluid retention by enhancing the activity of the kidneys and promoting the excretion of excess fluids and electrolytes.

Quercetin: A flavonoid phenolic compound that helps reduce fluid retention by inhibiting the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), which plays a key role in regulating blood pressure and fluid balance.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and improve fluid drainage.
Roots: Contain inulin, a prebiotic that helps regulate fluid balance and reduce swelling.
Flowers: High in saponins, which have a diuretic effect and help eliminate excess fluid from the body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in the morning after the dew has dried but before the heat of the day sets in.
Chop 20-30 grams of taraxacum officinale roots and 20-30 grams of taraxacum officinale leaves into small pieces to enhance extraction.
Combine the chopped taraxacum officinale roots and leaves with 500 milliliters of cold water in a saucepan and heat over low heat.
Simmer the taraxacum officinale mixture for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly and the flavors have melded together.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container to remove solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena decoctions helps with fluid retention because of its natural diuretic properties, which stimulate the kidneys to eliminate excess water from the body. The herb's bioactive compounds, such as verbascoside and lemongrass oil, work together to reduce swelling and alleviate the discomfort associated with fluid buildup in tissues and organs. By promoting urinary frequency and improving circulation, lemon verbena decoctions can help to normalize blood pressure and restore balance to the body's fluid levels, leaving individuals feeling lighter, more energized, and less bloated.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Lemon verbena decoctions stimulate urine production, which helps to increase the removal of excess fluids and electrolytes from the body, reducing swelling and discomfort associated with water retention.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon verbena's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce inflammation in the body, which is often a contributing factor to fluid retention, and may also alleviate symptoms such as bloating, pain, and stiffness.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in lemon verbena decoctions help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to fluid buildup and other systemic imbalances.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help reduce fluid retention by inhibiting the reabsorption of sodium and water in the kidneys, thereby increasing urine production and reducing swelling.

Phenolic Acids (e.g. Verbascoside): These compounds have diuretic properties, which help increase urine production and reduce fluid buildup in the body by stimulating the kidneys to remove excess fluids.

Flavonoids (e.g. Quercetin): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to fluid retention, making it easier for the body to eliminate excess fluids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce fluid retention.
Roots: They are used for their diuretic and expectorant properties, which aid in reducing fluid retention and promoting urination.
Stems: They are used due to their ability to stimulate diuresis, helping to reduce fluid retention and alleviate associated symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried verbena officinalis leaves and flowers in equal parts to make a decoction.
Combine 1 tablespoon of the mixture with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Store the strained decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with fluid retention because of its natural ability to balance the body's electrolyte levels. The licorice root contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has a diuretic effect, increasing urine production and reducing water retention in the body. Additionally, licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling caused by fluid buildup. By regulating fluid levels and alleviating inflammation, herbal licorice decoctions can provide effective relief from bloating, edema, and other symptoms associated with fluid retention.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Licorice root contains glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to inhibit the activity of enzymes involved in fluid retention and swelling, thereby reducing edema and bloating.

Flavonoids: The flavonoid compounds present in licorice decoctions have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and relax smooth muscle tissue, thereby alleviating fluid retention symptoms such as bloating and swelling.

Triterpenes: Licorice root contains triterpene saponins, which have been shown to possess diuretic and natriuretic (urine- increasing) properties, helping to reduce fluid buildup in the body by increasing urine production and excretion.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps with fluid retention by inhibiting the enzyme 11β-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase (11β-HSD), which is involved in the conversion of cortisol to its active form, thus reducing the levels of active cortisol in the body and decreasing the amount of water and electrolytes retained.

Licorice root flavonoids (mainly isoorientin and isovitisin): These flavonoids have a mild diuretic effect, increasing urine production and reducing water retention by improving kidney function and promoting the excretion of excess fluids.
Glycyrrhetinic acid: It has a mild mineralocorticoid activity, which helps to regulate electrolyte balance and reduce fluid retention by promoting the excretion of sodium and water in the urine.
remedy_parts: Roots: The root of Glycyrrhiza glabra is widely used to make decoctions for fluid retention due to its high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce water retention.

Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are sometimes used to make decoctions for fluid retention, although they are less commonly used than the roots, as they contain smaller amounts of glycyrrhizin.

Barks: The bark of Glycyrrhiza glabra may be used to make decoctions for fluid retention, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is believed to have diuretic properties and help to reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of clean dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra in late summer or early fall.
Boil 1 liter of water in a large pot and reduce the heat to a simmer.
Add the harvested roots to the simmering water and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid from the roots through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Allow the decoction to cool and then store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow decoctions helps with fluid retention because they contain mucilage, a thick, gel-like substance that coats the digestive tract and soothes inflammation. This natural coating reduces irritation in the body's tissues, allowing excess water to be absorbed and eliminated more effectively. As a result, marshmallow decoctions help reduce fluid buildup in the body, alleviating symptoms of edema, bloating, and other forms of swelling. By promoting healthy digestion and hydration, marshmallow decoctions provide natural relief from fluid retention.
remedy_properties: Demulcent action: Marshmallow decoctions create a soothing, protective barrier on the mucous membranes, allowing them to retain moisture and preventing excessive water loss, which can contribute to fluid retention.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and pain associated with fluid retention, helping to alleviate symptoms such as bloating, edema, and discomfort.
Diuretic action: Some marshmallow species have a mild diuretic effect, which can help increase urine production and flush out excess fluids retained in the body, further alleviating fluid retention.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help with fluid retention by forming a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and preventing excess fluid from entering the tissues.

Phenolic acids (especially Gallic acid): Phenolic acids, such as Gallic acid, help with fluid retention by inhibiting the activity of sodium-potassium pumps, which can contribute to fluid accumulation in the body.

Flavonoids (especially Quercetin): Flavonoids, such as Quercetin, help with fluid retention by reducing inflammation and preventing the breakdown of connective tissue, which can lead to fluid accumulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Althaea officinalis are often used due to their high mucilage content, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate fluid retention.
Leaves: The leaves of Althaea officinalis are also commonly used, as they contain similar mucilage properties that aid in soothing and reducing fluid buildup.

Stems: Although less commonly mentioned, the stems of Althaea officinalis may be used in some herbal preparations, potentially due to their secondary metabolites that contribute to the plant's medicinal properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-4 ounces of fresh root or 1-2 teaspoons of dried root of althaea officinalis.
Clean the root thoroughly with water to remove any dirt or debris.
Crush the root into small pieces to increase its surface area for decoction.
Combine the crushed root with 2 cups of water in a saucepan and heat gently over low heat for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower decoctions helps with fluid retention because of its ability to stimulate blood flow and improve lymphatic circulation. The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with fluid buildup. Additionally, the diuretic properties of Kansas coneflower can increase urine production, allowing the body to eliminate excess water and sodium more efficiently. By addressing these underlying causes of fluid retention, herbal Kansas coneflower decoctions provide natural relief from bloating, swelling, and other symptoms.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Kansas Coneflower decoctions have been shown to increase urine production, helping to remove excess fluids from the body and reduce swelling.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Kansas Coneflower may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the kidneys and urinary tract, which can contribute to fluid retention.

Antioxidant properties: Kansas Coneflower's antioxidant properties may help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to fluid retention and swelling by affecting blood vessel function and permeability.
remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: These compounds may help with fluid retention by reducing inflammation and improving the body's natural drainage and circulation processes.

Iridoid glycosides: Iridoid glycosides, such as echinacystic acid, may help reduce fluid retention by inhibiting the reabsorption of sodium in the kidneys, which can lead to increased urine production and reduced swelling.

Alkaloids: Although the specific alkaloids present in Echinacea angustifolia are not well-studied, some alkaloids found in other Echinacea species, such as echinatin, may help reduce fluid retention by relaxing the muscles in the kidneys and improving urine flow.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high concentration of inulin, which helps reduce fluid retention by promoting diuresis.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are also commonly used for their similar inulin content to roots, aiding in fluid reduction and diuretic effects.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their secondary diuretic properties, although less potent than roots and rhyzomes, they still contribute to reducing fluid retention.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30-60 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia root and separate it from other plant material.
Combine the echinacea root with 1 liter of water in a large pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the decoction steep for 10-15 minutes with the lid off.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before storing it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenrod decoctions helps with fluid retention because of its natural diuretic properties, which stimulate the kidneys to release excess water and salt from the body. The decoction's bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, also have anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with fluid buildup. Additionally, goldenrod's mild astringent properties help to tone and tighten the connective tissue, further reducing the appearance of puffiness and water retention.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Goldenrod decoction helps to increase urine production, reducing fluid retention by eliminating excess water and toxins from the body.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Goldenrod decoction reduce swelling and inflammation caused by fluid retention, alleviating discomfort and pain.

Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in Goldenrod decoction help to constrict blood vessels and reduce permeability, thereby reducing the amount of fluids that leak into tissues and causing edema.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, in Solidago virgaurea decoctions help with fluid retention by reducing inflammation and improving vascular health, thereby decreasing fluid accumulation in the body.

Triterpenes: Triterpenes, such as oleanolic acid and ursolic acid, in Solidago virgaurea decoctions help with fluid retention by inhibiting the activity of aldosterone, a hormone that promotes sodium retention and fluid accumulation in the body.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid, in Solidago virgaurea decoctions help with fluid retention by increasing urine production and reducing sodium reabsorption in the kidneys, thereby promoting the excretion of excess fluids from the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Solidago virgaurea are used to make decoctions for fluid retention due to their diuretic properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Solidago virgaurea are used to make decoctions for fluid retention due to their ability to stimulate urine production.
Stems: The stems of Solidago virgaurea are used to make decoctions for fluid retention due to their potential to help reduce water retention in the body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the solidago virgaurea roots and rhizomes in the fall after the plant has finished flowering.
Clean and dry the harvested roots and rhizomes to prevent contamination and spoilage.
Chop the dried roots and rhizomes into small pieces to increase their surface area.
Combine 1 part of the chopped roots and rhizomes with 4 parts of water in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has reduced to half its original volume.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horse chestnut decoctions helps with fluid retention because it is rich in aesculin, a compound that has a diuretic effect on the body. Aesculin helps to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which in turn reduces swelling and bloating caused by excess water retention. As the body eliminates excess fluids, it also reduces pressure on blood vessels, alleviating symptoms of conditions such as varicose veins and hemorrhoids. By promoting healthy fluid balance, horse chestnut decoctions can provide relief from uncomfortable swelling and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Aescin: Aescin, a saponin extracted from horse chestnut seeds, has potent diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce edema and alleviate symptoms of fluid retention by increasing urine production and improving blood circulation.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in horse chestnut extracts, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have antioxidant properties that help to stabilize capillaries and prevent excessive permeability, which can contribute to fluid buildup and edema.

Triterpenes: Triterpenes, a type of steroidal compound found in horse chestnut seeds, have been shown to possess mild diuretic and anti-inflammatory activities, helping to reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms of fluid retention.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins help with fluid retention by increasing the permeability of blood vessels, allowing excess fluid to be eliminated from the body.

Phenolic compounds (including flavonoids and phenolic acids): Phenolic compounds help with fluid retention by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow, which helps the body eliminate excess fluid.

Steroidal saponins (including aescin): Steroidal saponins, particularly aescin, help with fluid retention by reducing capillary permeability and improving the integrity of blood vessels, preventing fluid from leaking into tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Aesculus hippocastanum roots are often used in decoctions to alleviate fluid retention due to their high content of escin, a saponin that helps to reduce swelling.
Seeds: Horse Chestnut seeds are commonly used to make decoctions for fluid retention because they contain a combination of flavonoids and saponins that help to improve blood flow and reduce swelling.
Barks: The bark of Aesculus hippocastanum is sometimes used in decoctions to treat fluid retention due to its ability to reduce inflammation and improve circulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 aesculus hippocastanum leaves and bark from mature trees with no signs of disease or pests.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place or using a dehydrator at 40°c for 2 hours.
Grind 5 grams of the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Steep 1 gram of the powder in 200ml of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids and discard the residue.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein decoctions helps with fluid retention because its diuretic properties stimulate the kidneys to eliminate excess water from the body. The herb's flavonoids, saponins, and mucilages work together to relax the tissues and reduce inflammation in the body, allowing for easier absorption of fluids and reducing swelling in the extremities. Additionally, mullein's natural anti-inflammatory compounds help to ease discomfort associated with fluid retention, providing relief from bloating, cramps, and stiffness.
remedy_properties: Demulcent properties: Mullein's demulcent properties create a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, helping to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues, which can alleviate fluid retention caused by inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Mullein's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to fluid retention, making it an effective natural treatment for this condition.

Diuretic properties: Mullein has been traditionally used as a natural diuretic, helping to increase urine production and remove excess fluids and toxins from the body, which can alleviate fluid retention caused by kidney or liver issues.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds help reduce fluid retention by increasing the permeability of the cell membrane, allowing excess fluids to be eliminated from the body.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Verbascum thapsus decoctions help mitigate fluid retention by modulating the activity of the sodium-potassium pump, thereby reducing sodium retention in the body.
Triterpenes: Triterpenes have diuretic properties, helping to reduce fluid retention by increasing urine production and promoting the elimination of excess fluids from the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make decoctions for fluid retention as they contain compounds that help reduce swelling and edema.
Roots: The roots are used for their diuretic properties, helping to increase urine production and reduce fluid retention.
Stems: The stems of Verbascum thapsus are also used to make decoctions for fluid retention, possibly due to their ability to promote sweating and diuresis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbascum thapsus flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dry place for 7 to 10 days.
Combine 1 part dried verbascum thapsus with 2 parts water in a saucepan and bring to boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 10 to 15 minutes or until the liquid is reduced by half.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a clean container and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder decoctions helps with fluid retention because they are rich in bioactive compounds that have a diuretic effect, stimulating the kidneys to release excess water from the body. The anti-inflammatory properties of elderberry also help reduce swelling and edema, making it an effective remedy for conditions such as water retention, bloating, and puffiness. Additionally, elder decoctions can help regulate blood pressure and improve circulation, which further contributes to reducing fluid buildup in the body.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Elderflower decoction helps to increase urine production, which can alleviate symptoms of fluid retention by removing excess water from the body.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in elderflowers help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with fluid retention, making it an effective remedy for conditions like edema and bloating.
Antioxidant properties: Elderflower's antioxidant properties help protect the kidneys and liver from oxidative stress, which can contribute to fluid retention and related health issues.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help reduce fluid retention by exerting diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects, thereby improving kidney function and reducing swelling.
Phenolic acids: They have a diuretic effect, which helps in eliminating excess fluids from the body and reducing edema.
Isoflavones: They help reduce fluid retention by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can improve kidney function and reduce swelling.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and improve lymphatic drainage.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their diuretic properties, which help to increase urine production and reduce fluid retention.
Fruits: The fruits are used for their anthocyanins and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects, helping to alleviate fluid retention and improve overall health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh or 2 cups of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves.
Clean and chop the collected sambucus nigra material into smaller pieces.
Combine the chopped sambucus nigra with 4 cups of water in a saucepan.
Bring the sambucus nigra mixture to a boil then reduce the heat to a simmer.
Allow the sambucus nigra decoction to steep for 10 to 20 minutes then strain.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek decoctions helps with fluid retention because of its unique ability to stimulate blood circulation, improve lymphatic drainage, and reduce inflammation. The saponins present in fenugreek help to break down and eliminate excess fluids in the body, while also relaxing the muscles surrounding blood vessels and reducing blood pressure. This combination leads to a reduction in water accumulation in the tissues, resulting in relief from bloating, swelling, and discomfort associated with fluid retention.
remedy_properties: Diuretic property: Fenugreek decoction increases urine production, helping to flush out excess fluids and reduce swelling due to its ability to stimulate the kidneys to release more urine.

Anti-inflammatory property: Fenugreek contains flavonoids and saponins that have anti-inflammatory effects, which help reduce inflammation in the body and alleviate symptoms associated with fluid retention such as edema and pain.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant compounds present in fenugreek decoction help protect the kidneys from damage caused by free radicals, maintaining their function and reducing the risk of kidney stones or other complications related to fluid retention.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of fenugreek are effective in reducing fluid retention due to their diuretic properties, which help eliminate excess water from the body.
Seeds: Fenugreek seeds contain soluble fiber that helps reduce fluid retention by slowing down digestion and absorption of fluids into the bloodstream.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh fenugreek leaves or use dried ones measuring 10-15 grams for every 500 milliliters of water.
Combine the harvested fenugreek leaves or dried powder with boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 20-30 minutes or until the liquid reaches a comfortable temperature for consumption.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding solids and any excess sediment.
Store the cooled decoction in an airtight container at room temperature for up to two days before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with fluid retention because it is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and saponins that work synergistically to reduce inflammation and improve circulation. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's decoction helps reduce inflammation, which can contribute to fluid retention by alleviating conditions such as arthritis and gout.
Diuretic properties: The decoction stimulates the kidneys to increase urine production, helping to remove excess fluids and alleviate swelling associated with fluid retention.

Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic conditions like hypertension, a leading cause of fluid retention.

Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making it useful for reducing fluid buildup and alleviating edema.
remedy_recipe: Rinse 20-30 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with filtered water to remove any impurities.
Boil one quart of filtered water in a large pot and add the rinsed ginkgo biloba leaves.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 15-20 minutes to extract the active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids.
Cool the decoction to room temperature before refrigerating or freezing for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric decoctions helps with fluid retention because of its potent anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties. The bioactive compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to fluid buildup. Additionally, the decoction's warming and stimulating effects help to increase blood circulation, promoting the elimination of excess fluids through urine excretion. As a result, regular consumption of herbal turmeric decoctions may aid in reducing water retention, alleviating symptoms such as bloating, swelling, and discomfort associated with fluid imbalance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric's active compound curcumin reduces inflammation, which can contribute to fluid retention by improving blood vessel permeability and reducing edema formation.
Diuretic property: Turmeric's diuretic effect helps increase urine production, promoting the excretion of excess fluids and electrolytes that can cause swelling.

Antioxidant property: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and cellular damage, which can contribute to fluid retention by improving overall circulation and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of turmeric contain a high concentration of curcuminoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce water retention.
Roots: Turmeric roots are rich in fiber and antioxidants, making them effective at promoting digestive health and reducing fluid accumulation in the body.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried turmeric root curcuma longa and 2 cups of pure water.
Rinse the dried turmeric root with cold running water then chop it into small pieces.
Combine the chopped turmeric root with the 2 cups of pure water in a saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean cup.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary decoctions helps with fluid retention because of its natural diuretic properties, which stimulate the kidneys to excrete excess water from the body. The herb's essential oils also improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the lymphatic system, further aiding in the removal of toxins and excess fluids. Diuretic: The diuretic effect of rosemary stimulates urine production, allowing the body to eliminate excess fluids and toxins more efficiently, which can help alleviate symptoms of fluid retention such as swelling and puffiness.

Antioxidant: Rosemary's antioxidant properties help protect the kidneys from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to fluid retention, while also improving overall kidney function and promoting healthy urine production.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots have been traditionally used to reduce edema and swelling caused by excess water retention due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems of rosemary contain a high concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to improve circulation and reduce fluid accumulation in the body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried leaves of rosmarinus officinalis, weighing approximately 20-30 grams for each serving.
Combine the harvested leaves with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, ensuring the liquid reaches a temperature of 95°c to 100°c.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid portion.
Store the prepared decoction in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 24 hours or refrigerate it for up to 3 days before consumption.

remedy_recipe: Gather one ounce of dried equisetum arvense herb for every eight ounces of water.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Rinse 20-30 grams of dried or fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers gently with filtered water.
Combine rinsed flowers with 500 milliliters of boiling distilled water and steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Discard solids and use the resulting decoction as a warm beverage within 24 hours.
